mechanism: Each BoJ rate hike compresses the US-Japan rate differential by ~25-50bp, narrowing the carry cushion and raising the conditional probability of an unwind event. The normalization paradox means tightening itself generates the instability it seeks to prevent.
regime_dependence: Strongest when CFTC positioning is elevated (>100K contracts) and credit spreads are below danger thresholds, allowing positions to rebuild between hikes.

mechanism: A carry unwind would resolve the MOVE-FX vol divergence by repricing FX vol sharply higher. The current 1.8 sigma divergence represents stored energy that carry unwind would release.
regime_dependence: Resolution pathway is regime-dependent: in low-vol carry accumulation, divergence persists; transition to high-vol triggers convergence via FX vol spike.

mechanism: The rate-FX R-squared regime (8-12% in low vol vs 0-2% in high vol) maps directly to the Dollar Smile zones. Middle zone = low-vol regime where rate differentials drive FX. Left/right sides = high-vol where risk appetite or growth dominance overrides rates.
regime_dependence: Relationship is tautological in the middle zone and breaks down at extremes. Useful only when vol regime can be identified ex ante.

mechanism: Managed/pegged regimes that diverge from de jure classification accumulate misalignment that eventually produces 30-60% discrete devaluations, triggering the crisis archetype. The mismatch itself is one of the 5 scoring signals.
regime_dependence: Strongest in economies with limited reserve buffers and political constraints on preemptive adjustment. Weakened where reserves are sufficient (China $3T+) or where administrative controls dampen adjustment.

mechanism: In normal state, carry and momentum are diversifying (correlation -0.2 to -0.3). During carry unwind stress, correlation snaps to +0.5 to +0.9, amplifying losses by 25-40% beyond what normal-state VaR models predict.
regime_dependence: Switch activates during stress episodes; the diversification benefit exists only in calm markets when it is least needed.

mechanism: FCI ~75-100bp easier than policy rate creates an unstable equilibrium: if equity/credit channels reverse, conditions tighten sharply without any policy change, potentially forcing the dollar direction resolution.
regime_dependence: Relationship is asymmetric: equity/credit channels can tighten conditions much faster than monetary policy can ease them.

mechanism: CB gold purchases at 1000+ tonnes/year are a revealed preference for reserve diversification away from USD assets, compounding the 0.5-0.8pp/year reserve share decline. Post-Russia sanctions freeze accelerated the linkage.
regime_dependence: Strongest among non-Western central banks exposed to sanctions risk. Weakened if gold price appreciation reduces incentive for further allocation.

mechanism: If fiscal concerns trigger a US-origin risk-off event, the dollar could weaken alongside equities rather than strengthen, bifurcating the left tail of the Dollar Smile. Term premium expansion and declining auction bid-to-cover are early signals.
regime_dependence: Only relevant if fiscal concerns become the primary risk-off driver rather than traditional recession/geopolitical catalysts. No post-1971 US precedent.

mechanism: Dollar invoicing dominance (Gopinath) creates the co-movement channel: stress events trigger dollar demand for trade settlement across all currency pairs simultaneously, causing carry and safe-haven currencies to snap to co-movement.
regime_dependence: Permanent structural feature as long as dollar invoicing share remains dominant (~88% of FX turnover).

mechanism: 8-15% trade-weighted BEER overvaluation provides gravitational pull toward weakness, but the exorbitant privilege can delay mean-reversion for years. Overvaluation is a necessary but insufficient condition for cycle inflection.
regime_dependence: BEER mean-reversion has documented 1-5 year out-of-sample predictive power but zero short-term predictive power. Overvaluation can persist or widen before reversing.

mechanism: Twin deficits (fiscal 6-7% + current account 3-4%) create flow headwinds that reinforce BEER overvaluation. Higher deficits require more foreign capital inflow to sustain dollar level, creating a fragile equilibrium.
regime_dependence: Relationship weakened or negated when safe-asset demand is high (risk-off) and strengthened when growth convergence reduces relative US appeal.

mechanism: China's administered FX regime (de jure managed float, de facto crawling peg) accumulates a growing gap between administered and shadow equilibrium rates. This stored energy eventually requires discontinuous adjustment, as in 2015.
regime_dependence: China's $3T+ reserves provide a much larger buffer than pre-crisis Asian pegs, extending the duration stored energy can accumulate before release.

mechanism: Fiscal expansion in non-recessionary economy reshapes the smile into an asymmetric smirk, structurally supporting the dollar in the middle zone and potentially preventing the weakening that growth convergence would otherwise produce.
regime_dependence: Only applies during sustained fiscal expansion. A fiscal consolidation or recession would remove the smirk effect.

mechanism: Dollar direction resolution will create large mechanical impacts on S&P 500 reported earnings (2-4% EPS per 10% DXY move), with non-GAAP adjustments amplifying or obscuring the effect depending on direction.
regime_dependence: Always present for multinationals with >30% international revenue; magnitude scales linearly with DXY move size.

mechanism: Persistent capital inflows driven by safe-asset demand may offset twin deficit pressure, potentially rendering threshold-based analysis meaningless. The privilege acts as a counterweight to fundamental headwinds.
regime_dependence: Strongest when global risk appetite is low and alternative safe assets are scarce. Weakens during periods of active reserve diversification.
